goal: Make a browser game inspired by classic DOOM: a fast first-person maze shooter using HTML/JavaScript/Canvas. Core gameplay: - First-person pseudo-3D raycast engine with textured walls, floor, ceiling, doors, and dark corridors. - Player moves with arrow keys or WASD: forward/back/strafe left/strafe right relative to facing direction. - Mouse steers left/right. Ignore vertical aim for movement. - Add a minimap in the corner showing maze walls, player position, and a cone/arrow showing viewing direction. - Gun is fixed at bottom center of screen. The END OF THE GUN must point exactly to the screen center/crosshair X. - When firing, muzzle flash must appear exactly at the gun barrel tip/screen center line, not off to the side. - Left mouse fires. Add recoil, short flash animation, sound hook, and bullet hit detection. Monsters: - High-resolution animated monsters, not simple blobs. - Each monster needs idle, walking, attacking, pain, and death animation frames. - Monsters should face the player visually, move through the maze, avoid walls, chase when they see/hear the player, and attack up close or at range. - Animate monster movement smoothly with frame cycling and slight body motion. - Scale monsters correctly by distance so they look large up close and small far away. Level: - Build a maze map with rooms, corridors, doors, health/ammo pickups, and exit. - Use collision detection for walls, monsters, and pickups. - Add health, ammo, score, enemy count, and level complete screen. Graphics: - Make graphics as high resolution as possible for the canvas size. - Use detailed wall textures, lighting falloff with distance, shadows, muzzle flash glow, blood/hit effects, and animated enemies. - Prioritize smooth gameplay and correct perspective over fancy code structure. Deliver one complete working HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript. No external assets required; generate placeholder sprites/textures in code or with canvas drawing, but make them detailed and animated.
